project_type: Full-stack TypeScript application - Next.js frontend with Express backend API
build_system: npm with separate frontend and backend packages in a monorepo structure
test_framework: Jest for unit and integration tests, React Testing Library for frontend component tests
build_command: npm run build (in both frontend/ and backend/ directories)
test_command: npm test (in both frontend/ and backend/ directories)
verification_instructions: 1. Run 'npm install' in backend/ and frontend/. 2. Run 'npm test' in backend/ to verify API and business logic tests pass. 3. Run 'npm test' in frontend/ to verify component tests pass. 4. Run 'npm run build' in both directories to verify compilation.
snapshot_or_generated_files: Prisma generates client code from schema - run 'npx prisma generate' after schema changes
setup_instructions: 1. cd backend && npm install && npx prisma generate && npx prisma db push. 2. cd frontend && npm install.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - full network access available. Node.js 22 via nvm. SQLite for local development database (no PostgreSQL server needed). External services (TOS, Payment, Notification, Slot, Validation) are stubbed with interfaces.
contribution_requirements: Greenfield project - follow the architecture from design.md. Modular service pattern. REST API endpoints as specified. Complete audit trail on all appointment mutations.
key_patterns: Domain-driven design with service layer pattern. Prisma ORM for database access. Express Router for API routes. React components with hooks for frontend. Stubbed external service interfaces for future integration.
directory_structure: Monorepo with backend/ (Express + Prisma + SQLite) and frontend/ (Next.js + React). backend/src/routes/ for API endpoints, backend/src/services/ for business logic, backend/src/middleware/ for auth/validation, backend/prisma/ for schema and migrations, backend/tests/ for test files. frontend/src/app/ for Next.js pages, frontend/src/components/ for React components, frontend/src/lib/ for API client and utilities.
